primary_complaint: Right shoulder pain and weakness following a work-related lifting injury in June 2024
functional_limitations: Unable to lift objects above shoulder height
Cannot perform overhead reaching for daily tasks
Disturbed sleep due to shoulder pain
Unable to return to work duties requiring arm elevation
objective_measurements: Active forward flexion: 95 degrees (limited by pain)
Strength testing: 3/5 supraspinatus on empty can test

conservative_treatments_attempted.outcome: minimal improvement, pain persisted with overhead activity
2 weeks of partial relief, then return to baseline symptoms
inadequate pain control, GI intolerance developed

imaging_findings.key_findings: X-ray (01/22/2025): AC joint arthrosis Grade II. No calcific deposits. No significant glenohumeral arthrosis.; MRI (01/22/2025): Full-thickness supraspinatus tear measuring 2.1 cm with retraction to the musculotendinous junction. Moderate subacromial bursitis. Intact subscapularis.

requested_procedure: Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair, right shoulder — CPT 29827
surgical_approach_if_mentioned: Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair

denial_risk_flags.label: Physical Therapy Duration Below Typical Threshold
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Physical therapy was documented for only 8 weeks. Many BCBS policies for CPT 29827 require a minimum of 6–12 weeks of formal PT, but some regional plans require documentation of 12 weeks or a formal discharge summary confirming failure.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a PT discharge summary or attending note confirming that 8 weeks of structured PT was completed and resulted in documented failure, and clarify whether any additional home exercise program was continued beyond formal PT.

denial_risk_flags.label: Corticosteroid Injection Duration Not Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The corticosteroid injection entry does not include a treatment duration or a follow-up interval, only an administration date of 11/05/2024. Payers may flag incomplete injection records as insufficient evidence of conservative care failure.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a follow-up note dated after 11/05/2024 documenting the patient's return to baseline symptoms and confirming the injection trial is considered failed conservative management.

denial_risk_flags.label: Attending Surgeon Name Is a Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The attending surgeon is listed as Dr. Michael Torres, which is a placeholder token. Payers require a clearly identified requesting and operating provider for prior authorization of CPT 29827.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Confirm and populate the attending surgeon's full name and NPI before submission to avoid automatic denial due to missing provider identification.

denial_risk_flags.label: Injury Date Is a Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The work-related injury onset date is listed as June 2024, a placeholder token. Payers may require a specific injury date to validate medical necessity timelines and workers' compensation coordination.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Populate the exact injury date in place of June 2024 to establish a clear clinical timeline from injury onset through conservative care failure to surgical request.

denial_risk_flags.label: Facility Name Is a Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The practice is listed as Summit Orthopedic Group, a placeholder token. Payers may require a verified facility name and address for prior authorization processing.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Replace Summit Orthopedic with the full legal practice name and include the facility NPI and address in the submission.

diagnosis_codes.note: Two ICD-10 codes are documented (M75.121 and M75.111), clearly supporting the diagnosis of full-thickness rotator cuff tear and shoulder impingement.

conservative_treatments_named.note: Three distinct conservative treatments are named: physical therapy, corticosteroid injection (Kenalog 40mg), and NSAIDs (Meloxicam 15mg daily).

conservative_treatment_duration.note: Physical therapy duration (8 weeks) and NSAID duration (3 months) are explicitly stated; the corticosteroid injection lacks a duration but the other two entries provide adequate duration documentation.

imaging_findings.note: Both MRI and X-ray are documented as completed with specific findings including a 2.1 cm full-thickness supraspinatus tear confirmed on MRI dated 01/22/2025.

functional_limitations.note: Four specific functional limitations are explicitly documented, including inability to perform overhead work, disturbed sleep, and inability to return to work duties.

surgical_approach.note: Surgical approach is explicitly stated as arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 29827 (arthroscopic rotator cuff repair) is a valid and appropriate code for the documented diagnosis of full-thickness rotator cuff tear.

symptom_duration.note: Symptom duration is explicitly stated as 8 months of conservative management failure in the medical necessity statement.
